name: Ahmedabad
description: Ahmedabad is a magnificent destination in Gujarat, India. Known for its remarkable blend of history, culture, and natural beauty. Visitors flock here to experience its unique atmosphere, vibrant local markets, and iconic landmarks. Whether you are seeking spiritual peace, thrilling adventures, or simply a relaxing holiday, Ahmedabad offers a memorable experience that captures the true essence of Indian heritage. The city's charm lies in its timeless appeal and warm hospitality.
history: Ahmedabad boasts a rich history spanning several centuries, serving as a prominent center for trade, culture, and power under various dynasties.

topAttractions.name: Sabarmati Ashram (Gandhi Ashram)
topAttractions.description: Mahatma Gandhi lived here from 1917-1930. From here he launched the Salt March (Dandi March) on March 12, 1930. The museum contains his personal belongings, letters, and a diorama of the Salt March. One of the most moving museums in India. Entry free. Open daily 8:30AM-6:30PM.

topAttractions.name: Adalaj Step Well (Vav)
topAttractions.description: A stunning 5-storey octagonal step well (1498 CE) built by Queen Rudabai of the Vaghela dynasty, blending Hindu and Islamic architectural elements. The well descends 4 storeys with intricately carved walls, pavilions, and serpentine designs. Best in morning light when sun illuminates the depths. Entry: Free.

topAttractions.name: Sidi Saiyyed Mosque (Jali Windows)
topAttractions.description: A 1573 mosque famous for its extraordinary stone jali (lattice) windows depicting the Tree of Life — intertwining branches and leaves in detailed stone carving. The image of these windows became the logo of the Indian Institute of Management Ahmedabad. One of the finest examples of stone lattice work in the world.

topAttractions.name: Akshardham Temple Gandhinagar
topAttractions.description: A magnificent Hindu temple complex (1992) in the neighboring state capital Gandhinagar (23km), featuring 97m-long carvings of over 200 scenes from Indian scriptures, 9 domes, 208 ornamental pillars. The spiritual complex also includes an exhibition on the life of Swaminarayan.

topAttractions.name: Calico Museum of Textiles
topAttractions.description: One of the finest textile museums in the world — housing 6 centuries of Indian textiles including Mughal royal fabrics, temple textiles, and regional weaving traditions. By appointment only (morning and afternoon sessions). Entry: Free by appointment. Considered the most important textile collection in Asia.

topAttractions.name: Kankaria Lake
topAttractions.description: A 15th-century artificial lake (1451 CE) built by Sultan Qutbuddin Ahmedshah II, 2km in circumference. The Nagina Wadi island in the center houses a zoo. A popular recreational spot with a heritage walk around the perimeter, toy train, and Balvatika children's park.

culture: The culture of Ahmedabad is a vibrant tapestry of traditional arts, festivals, and culinary delights, deeply influenced by its historical roots.
travelTips: Book accommodations in advance during peak season
Try the local street food but ensure it is hot and fresh
Use authorized taxis or rideshare apps
Respect local customs and dress modestly at religious sites

itineraries.name: Highlights Tour
activities: Day 1: Arrival, City Center Attractions, Evening Market
Day 2: Historical Sites, Local Food Tasting, Departure
